---
title: Cross compiling 32 bit R packages on 64 bit Raspberry Pi OS
author: Package Build
date: '2023-03-06'
slug: cross-compiling-r-packages-on-raspberry-pi-os
categories:
  - Blog
tags:
  - R
  - Linux
  - Raspberry Pi
subtitle: ''
summary: ''
authors: []
lastmod: '2023-03-02T14:57:41Z'
featured: no
image:
  caption: ''
  focal_point: ''
  preview_only: no
projects: []
---

<https://wiki.debian.org/Multiarch/HOWTO>

arm64 armhf

Slightly confusingly R reports arm64 as aarch64, and armhf as armv7l.

```bash
sudo dpkg --add-architecture armhf
sudo apt-get update
```

For libjpeg9 to `/etc/apt/sources.list` add
```bash
deb http://ftp.uk.debian.org/debian sid main
```
```bash
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get upgrade
```

This took a long time.

```bash
sudo apt-get install libicu67:armhf
```

```bash
sudo apt-get install -y g++ gcc gfortran libjpeg9 make:armhf
sudo apt-get install -y r4pi:armhf
```

An R session can then be launched with
```bash
R --arch=armhf
```
or by specifying the full path to the executable.

```
pi@raspberrypi:~/Downloads/rstan $ R

R version 4.2.2 (2022-10-31) -- "Innocent and Trusting"
Copyright (C) 2022 The R Foundation for Statistical Computing
Platform: armv7l-unknown-linux-gnueabihf (32-bit)

R is free software and comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY.
You are welcome to redistribute it under certain conditions.
Type 'license()' or 'licence()' for distribution details.

  Natural language support but running in an English locale

R is a collaborative project with many contributors.
Type 'contributors()' for more information and
'citation()' on how to cite R or R packages in publications.

Type 'demo()' for some demos, 'help()' for on-line help, or
'help.start()' for an HTML browser interface to help.
Type 'q()' to quit R.

> 
```

R reports armhf as armv7l.

```
> R.Version()$arch
[1] "armv7l"
> version
               _                             
platform       armv7l-unknown-linux-gnueabihf
arch           armv7l                        
os             linux-gnueabihf               
system         armv7l, linux-gnueabihf       
status                                       
major          4                             
minor          2.2                           
year           2022                          
month          10                            
day            31                            
svn rev        83211                         
language       R                             
version.string R version 4.2.2 (2022-10-31)  
nickname       Innocent and Trusting        
```

```bash
curl https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/rstan_2.21.8.tar.gz
tar -zxvf rstan_2.21.8.tar.gz
```

```r
install.packages('devtools')
devtools::build(binary = TRUE, args = c('--preclean'))
```

Failed with the following message
```
  /home/pi/R/armv7l-unknown-linux-gnueabihf-library/4.2/BH/include/boost/spirit/include/phoenix_core.hpp:12:1: note: in expansion of macro ‘BOOST_HEADER_DEPRECATED’
      12 | BOOST_HEADER_DEPRECATED("<boost/phoenix/core.hpp>")
         | ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
   virtual memory exhausted: Cannot allocate memory
   make: *** [/opt/R/release/lib/R/etc/Makeconf:178: grammar_inst.o] Error 1
   ERROR: compilation failed for package ‘rstan’
─  removing ‘/tmp/RtmpxIA0cN/temp_libpathdc8e22e5715c/rstan’
Error in `(function (command = NULL, args = character(), error_on_status = TRUE, …`:
! System command 'R' failed
---
Exit status: 1
stdout & stderr: <printed>
---
Type .Last.error to see the more details.

```

Need a swap file, as per <https://pimylifeup.com/raspberry-pi-swap-file/>

```bash
sudo dphys-swapfile swapoff
sudo nano /etc/dphys-swapfile
```
```
CONF_SWAPSIZE=1024
```
```bash
sudo dphys-swapfile setup
sudo dphys-swapfile swapon
sudo reboot
```
